skill.name: turborepo
skill.description: Guides Turborepo monorepo setup, task pipelines, caching, filtering, environment variables, CI, package boundaries, and remote cache configuration.
skill.summary: Configure and optimize Turborepo monorepos with practical task, cache, environment, and CI guidance.
skill.author: vercel

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, filesystem, network, environment, and credential patterns, but review found these are Markdown guidance and configuration examples for Turborepo. No prompt injection, malicious exfiltration, or executable payload was found; the remaining risk is that users or agents may run legitimate package manager and Turborepo commands or configure secrets incorrectly.

medium_findings.title: Legitimate External Command Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill contains many Turborepo, package manager, and CI command examples. They are expected for a build-system skill, but users should review commands before execution because they can install packages, run builds, or authenticate tooling.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are present and semantically relevant to Turborepo usage. I found no dynamic command construction or malicious command payloads.

medium_findings.title: Secret and Environment Variable Handling Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill documents DATABASE_URL, API_KEY, GITHUB_TOKEN, NPM_TOKEN, SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN, TURBO_TOKEN, and related variables. This is legitimate Turborepo guidance, but incorrect pass-through or cache configuration could expose secrets to tasks or create stale builds.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files explicitly discuss secret-like environment variable names and remote cache credentials. They are placeholders and best-practice examples, not evidence of credential theft.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal Findings Are Documentation Warnings
low_findings.description: The ../ patterns appear in examples that tell users not to traverse out of packages and to use $TURBO_ROOT$ instead. This is a defensive documentation pattern rather than path traversal logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced text labels ../ usage as wrong and provides a safer replacement. No filesystem access API or runtime path join is present in these locations.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto and Windows SAM Findings Are Text Matches
low_findings.description: Weak cryptography and Windows SAM findings appear to be keyword matches in Markdown headings, descriptions, or ordinary words. Review found no cryptographic implementation and no attempt to access the Windows SAM database.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown front matter, Turborepo dependency syntax, or remote cache option documentation. I did not find code that implements weak cryptography or reads sensitive operating system stores.

low_findings.title: No Prompt Injection Attempt Found
low_findings.description: Targeted review for override language, fake system instructions, pre-approval claims, and analysis-skipping instructions found no evidence of prompt injection in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill front matter and command file describe normal activation and routing behavior. No suspicious instruction override language was found during targeted review.

dangerous_patterns.title: Command Examples in Markdown
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes runnable shell commands such as npx turbo login, npx turbo link, turbo-ignore, and turbo build variants. These should be treated as user-approved operational actions, not automatic execution instructions.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are direct and runnable, but they are standard Turborepo workflows. No obfuscation, destructive flags, or exfiltration endpoint was identified.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote Cache and Token Configuration
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill explains remote cache endpoints and tokens, including TURBO_TOKEN and TURBO_TEAM. This is normal for Turborepo remote caching but requires careful secret handling in CI.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The token names and remote cache URL configuration are explicit. The text does not instruct users to disclose or transmit secrets to an untrusted service.

content.value_statement: Turborepo setup can be hard when tasks, cache inputs, filters, and CI settings interact. This skill gives focused guidance for reliable monorepo builds and faster pipelines.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to define package-level tasks and register them in turbo.json.
Guides cache configuration with outputs, inputs, env, globalEnv, and globalDependencies.
Provides filter and affected-package patterns for local development and CI.
Documents remote cache setup, token variables, and artifact signing options.
Helps structure internal packages and avoid common monorepo dependency mistakes.
Covers Turborepo CI patterns for GitHub Actions and Vercel deployments.
limitations: It provides guidance and examples; it does not verify a repository automatically.
Commands may install packages, authenticate services, or run builds if a user chooses to execute them.
It assumes JavaScript or TypeScript monorepo conventions with Turborepo.
Remote cache and secret handling still require project-specific security review.

use_cases.title: Set Up a New Monorepo
use_cases.description: Create package scripts, root turbo.json tasks, cache outputs, and workspace structure for a new JavaScript monorepo.

use_cases.title: Improve CI Build Speed
use_cases.description: Use affected-package filters, remote caching, and CI-specific Turborepo commands to reduce unnecessary work.

use_cases.title: Debug Cache Misses
use_cases.description: Identify missing outputs, incorrect inputs, environment variable issues, and task dependency mistakes that break caching.

prompt_templates.title: Create Basic Tasks
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for an existing workspace
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me add Turborepo build, lint, and test tasks for my apps and packages. Explain which files should change and why.

prompt_templates.title: Fix Cache Configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Turborepo cache behavior. Check outputs, inputs, env variables, and dependsOn settings for likely cache misses.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize CI Filters
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a CI plan that runs only affected Turborepo packages while still testing dependents and shared packages correctly.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Secure Remote Cache
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me configure Turborepo remote caching for local development and CI. Include token handling, signing, and failure modes.

output_examples.input: My build task runs every package and cache misses often.
output_examples.output: A diagnosis that separates task dependency issues, missing output declarations, broad environment inputs, and affected-package filter choices.

output_examples.input: I need GitHub Actions for a pnpm Turborepo workspace.
output_examples.output: A CI outline covering dependency install, affected builds, affected tests, cache variables, and safe secret placement.

output_examples.input: My app needs shared UI and environment-specific builds.
output_examples.output: A monorepo structure recommendation with package dependencies, task registration, environment variables, and cache input guidance.

best_practices: Put task logic in package scripts and let the root package.json delegate through turbo run.
Declare outputs, inputs, and environment variables so cache behavior matches real build inputs.
Review generated commands before execution, especially commands that install packages or configure remote cache authentication.
anti_patterns: Do not place build, lint, or test implementation logic directly in the root package.json.
Do not use broad .env patterns globally when only specific packages need those variables.
Do not pass secrets through build tasks unless they are required at runtime and do not affect outputs.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps configure Turborepo tasks, caching, filtering, CI workflows, remote cache, package structure, and environment variables.

faq.question: Does it execute Turborepo commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples. Users should review and approve operational commands before running them.

faq.question: Can it help with cache misses?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers outputs, inputs, environment variables, dependency relationships, and diagnostic commands for cache behavior.

faq.question: Does it support remote caching?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains Vercel Remote Cache, self-hosted cache variables, tokens, team settings, and artifact signing.

faq.question: Is it only for Vercel projects?
faq.answer: No. It includes Vercel guidance, but most task, cache, filter, and workspace patterns apply to Turborepo projects generally.

faq.question: What security review is still needed?
faq.answer: Review any generated CI changes, package manager commands, environment variable lists, and remote cache settings before applying them.
